messages.content: Write xorshift random number generator in assembler x86

Here is an example implementation of an xorshift random number generator in x86 assembly language:

```
section .data

; seed value
seed dd 0x12345678

section .text

global random

random:
  ; load seed value into eax
  mov eax, [seed]

  ; xorshift algorithm
  xor eax, 0x1f4ac56b
  shl eax, 13
  xor eax, 0xe84b0c6d
  shr eax, 17

  ; store updated seed value
  mov [seed], eax

  ; return random number in eax
  ret
```

This implementation initializes a seed value of `0x12345678` in the `.data` section. The `random` function loads this seed value into `eax`, performs the xorshift algorithm to update the seed value and generate a new random number, and then stores the updated seed value back into the `.data` section. The function then returns the random number in `eax`. 

Note that this implementation uses the standard x86 calling convention, where the function returns its result in `eax`. If you are using a different calling convention, the code will need to be adapted accordingly.
Adapt this code for thiscall convention

Here's an adaptation of the xorshift random number generator code for the `thiscall` calling convention, which is used by Microsoft Visual C++ compilers for non-static member functions:

```
; assume that the 'seed' instance variable has already been set up in an object
; caller is responsible for setting up the 'this' pointer correctly

public random         ; make the function public so that it can be called from C++

random:
  ; save callee-saved registers to the stack
  push ebp
  mov ebp, esp
  push ebx
  push esi
  push edi

  ; load 'seed' instance variable into eax
  mov eax, [ecx].seed

  ; xorshift algorithm
  xor eax, 0x1f4ac56b
  shl eax, 13
  xor eax, 0xe84b0c6d
  shr eax, 17

  ; store updated 'seed' value back into object
  mov [ecx].seed, eax

  ; restore callee-saved registers from stack
  pop edi
  pop esi
  pop ebx
  mov esp, ebp
  pop ebp

  ; return random number in eax
  ret
```

In this version of the function, the `this` pointer is passed in the `ecx` register, which is preserved across function calls by the `thiscall` calling convention. The instance variable `seed` is accessed using the `[ecx]` syntax.

The function saves the callee-saved registers (`ebx`, `esi`, `edi`) to the stack before doing any work, and restores them before returning. This ensures that the function preserves the caller's register values.

Finally, the function returns the random number in `eax`.
